title: URL Allowlisting, Untrusted API Data, Terminal Safety, Files, and Resource Abuse · Runtime Contract: Pokedex Go File Path Policy
summary: The client follows redirects anywhere, prints control characters from remote data, accepts arbitrary save paths, logs full responses, and lets crafted records exhaust memory or terminal output. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go file path policy increment in deny-by-default Pokedex trust-boundary policy. Learners progress throu…
objectives: Choose application-owned storage roots, reject traversal or unexpected file types, and minimize permission and symlink assumptions.
Normalize or visibly escape control, bidi, and ANSI sequences from API, file, and user data before terminal presentation.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
